summary: Most requirements are implemented correctly. REQ-1 through REQ-4 and REQ-6 are fully addressed. REQ-5 is partially addressed but the admin upload flow introduces Internet Identity (II) authentication as an additional step beyond what was specified. The II authentication layer is a hallucinated feature not requested by the spec.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity (II) authentication flow integrated into admin login after credential validation. After checking username/password, the app triggers an II login and assigns an admin role via actor call. This multi-step authentication was not requested.
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-4 only requires validating against hardcoded credentials and showing an upload section on success. No Internet Identity or backend role assignment was specified.

hallucinated_features.description: Backend actor role assignment via assignCallerUserRole called during admin login flow, involving a blockchain/canister interaction not mentioned in any requirement.
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec explicitly states uploads remain client-side/in-memory and no new backend API endpoints should be added. Using a backend actor to assign admin roles exceeds the stated scope.
